description: The assistant infers an incorrect fact from context, the user corrects it, and the correction itself is later refined. Tests whether the system distinguishes user-stated facts from assistant inferences and tracks a chain of corrections.

messages.content: I just got back from a two-week trip to Tokyo.
Welcome back! That must have been a great vacation in Japan.
By the way, it wasn't a vacation. I was there for a work conference.
Apologies for the assumption. Noted that your Tokyo trip was for a work conference.
To be more precise, it was a client engagement, not a conference. I was on-site with our Tokyo office for two weeks.
Got it. Your Tokyo trip was a two-week client engagement at your company's Tokyo office.
My manager asked me to write a trip summary. What do you know about my Tokyo trip?
Your Tokyo trip was a two-week client engagement at your company's Tokyo office, from around late February to early March 2026.
Can you remind me what my recent Tokyo trip was about?

memory_events.value: two-week client engagement at Tokyo office

previous_values.value: two-week client engagement at Tokyo office

probe.prompt: Can you remind me what my recent Tokyo trip was about?

ground_truth.current_value: two-week client engagement at Tokyo office

value_history.value: two-week client engagement at Tokyo office

interference.description: The assistant's initial inference ('vacation') was never stated by the user. Systems that store assistant inferences as user facts may return the wrong value even after correction.
